error.regex: (error CS\d{4}|Assets.*\.cs\(\d+.*error|Compilation failed|All compiler errors have to be fixed|scripts have compiler errors)

verdict.summary: C# script has a syntax or type error preventing compilation. Unity cannot enter Play mode until all compiler errors are fixed.

dead_ends.action: Delete Library folder to force recompilation
dead_ends.why_fails: Library folder is just a cache. If the script has a syntax error, recompiling produces the same error.

dead_ends.action: Reimport All assets to fix script errors
dead_ends.why_fails: Reimport is for asset pipeline issues, not C# syntax errors. Does not recompile scripts.

workarounds.action: Read the exact error code and line number in the Console
workarounds.how: Double-click the error in Console to jump to the exact line in your IDE

workarounds.action: Check for IDE/Unity version mismatch with .NET API compatibility
workarounds.how: Edit > Project Settings > Player > Api Compatibility Level: .NET Standard 2.1 or .NET Framework

workarounds.action: Ensure all referenced packages are installed and up to date
workarounds.how: Window > Package Manager > check for errors or missing packages referenced in your scripts
